title: goal-run-log-entry
description: Schema for ONE JSON Lines (JSONL) entry in a goal-run harness's durable run log (issue #874, plan step 1, AC2). The run log itself is a JSONL file -- each line is validated independently against this schema, not the file as a whole. Entry types are deliberately checkpoint | deviation | experience-observation | halt-claim -- NOT 'terminal': release detection consumes the platform's own goal_status transcript event (see goal-run-status-core.ps1) instead of a run-log terminal entry, per the goal-loop-capability-probe finding recorded in Documents/Design/goal-loop-capability-probe.md. Closed schema (additionalProperties: false) at the top level; per-type required fields are enforced via allOf/if/then rather than a oneOf split so every legal field is declared once in one flat properties block.

schema_version.description: The accepted schema_version set. Callers read this enum rather than hard-coding accepted versions.

issue.description: The GitHub issue number the goal-run harness invocation belongs to.

type.description: The four legal run-log entry types. 'terminal' is deliberately absent -- see this schema's top-level description.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this entry was written.

commit_sha.description: Required on checkpoint entries: the commit this checkpoint corresponds to.

summary.description: Short human-readable summary. Required on checkpoint, deviation, and halt-claim entries.

rationale.description: Required on deviation entries: why the executor deviated from the plan.

scenario.description: Required on experience-observation entries: which CE Gate scenario this observation belongs to.

observation.description: Required on experience-observation entries: what was observed.

halt_reason.description: Optional; only meaningful on halt-claim entries. Lets the executor distinguish, e.g., a genuine invariant-conflict from an unachievable-target claim before the harness's own halt-reason precedence logic (a later #874 step, not implemented here) runs. Reuses the same five-value enum as goal-halt-report.schema.json's halt_reason.
